beauty-enhanced-introducing-a-game-chang: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Many of you will know the brand’s beauty clay mask range – cult products that purify and soften the skin with impressive results that are matched by the sales figures. With over 6 million masks sold, these little foil-topped capsules are firm favorites with beauty enthusiasts and women all over the world. Ideal for popping in your overnight bag, you can take them on that business trip and turn an evening in the hotel into an indulgent mini-facial beauty experience.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61940" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] I first started working with Minenssey back in 2019, assisting the team with new product development , communications, and strategy. I absolutely loved the chance to be able to collaborate with a brand that focused on active botanicals and advocated the use of potent Australian natives such as Tasmanian Pepperberry and Flannel Flower for beauty enhancement while also being infused with the latest scientific advances. Fast forward four years, and we’ve kept these hero ingredients for their beauty benefits while also introducing the latest in “skinnovations” in the beauty industry.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61943" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Minenssey’s freshly upgraded clay mask series: the Minenssey Skin Revival Plus Vegan Collagen Mask Set, is the world’s first next generation collagen x clay face mask. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
skincare-success-my-eight-week-transform: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] As a result, I have to really cherry-pick the products I use, and I am rarely able to test a whole range in its entirety. Except in a recent instance when I completely flouted my usual rule! I went to a skincare launch and was so impressed that I immediately went home and shelved all the skincare I was currently using and replaced it with a full range of products. The range in question? Dr. Garry Cussell’s new Rejuvaus range. Here’s why… [/vc_column_text][vc_single_image image="61968" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
airgarden: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="63248" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Like many disruptors, the Bauer siblings were fueled by their own experiences and challenges that morphed into a deep-rooted commitment to change the narrative of modern-day food consumption. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] "Aside from being abject failures at trying to grow our own food in the soil, our Mum was probably the largest part of our inspiration," says Prue, co-founder of Airgarden . ”Mum was diagnosed with Alzheimers and Dementia and I had also had my own health challenges. We started looking at functional medicine, and the more we looked into it the more obvious it became that a big part of the issue was our modern-day diet and lifestyle.” [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="63344" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] The further they dug the more motivated Prue and Tom became and set about researching a product that would revolutionise home gardening. Trawling the web, they stumbled across aeroponics technology and learned how NASA grow fresh food for astronauts in space. At first, they were determined to bring this innovation to Australia. However, when importing existing systems proved costly and unsuitable for the Australian climate, they decided to make their own. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="63257" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] "If only we knew how fun overlaying manufacturing and horticulture was we may have never started! Especially given our backgrounds were in property and financial services," Prue points out blithely. Their mission was clear: make aeroponic gardening accessible and tailored to the Australian way of life. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="63252" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
bathing-a-love-affair: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]The product offering is no doubt a big part of the appeal. In this age of Self Care Sundays and Me-Time Mantras , bathing is enjoying a Renaissance. Here, the 7 products that will elevate your bathing experience to new heights of self-indulgence.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
lipstick-tattoo: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="63896" img_size="full" alignment="center"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] Preparing for the Procedure: On the day of the appointment, my tattoo artist, Katerina, first cleans my lips to ensure it is free of any makeup or residue, allowing for optimal pigment retention. She asked me to bring in my favourite lipstick for inspiration, so we could match the pigment as closely as possible to the product! I tell her I don’t want a strong pigment, just a very natural tint . I also request a gradient lip effect, where the pigment on the outer portion of my lips is lighter, and subtly blends into a darker colour towards the centre of the lips. Katerina mixes a few different shades together and shows me how the pigment will come together on a nude piece of paper. She holds each pigment blend against my skin and asks if we need to adjust any details to best compliment my skin tone. We trial multiple colour combinations and finally find the perfect blend – a peachy pink tint. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="63898" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
kerala-travel-guide: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] The retreat is still asleep. Except for me. [/vc_column_text][vc_single_image image="66208" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] The night air is thick—soupy, fragrant, alive with a heady blend of damp earth, salt, and distant spices. The scent of burning incense and ghee-fried dosa drifts up from the village below my clifftop vantage point. I take a sip of warm ginger tea and swat away a mozzie from my leg, still scented with sesame oil. This is Somatheeram, one of Kerala’s oldest Ayurvedic health resorts, and for the next few days, I will submit myself to an ancient healing tradition that has existed for over 5,000 years. I expect serenity. What I hadn’t vouched for is an all out assault on the senses . [/vc_column_text][vc_single_image image="66187" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
hypochlorous-acid-why-this-dermal-mist-h: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] This was particularly lucky because that very week the product had gone viral. So viral in fact, it sold out. (Don’t worry, it’s back in stock as of our publishing date.) But here’s why people are spritzing their hearts out, and why this is more than just another mist moment. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66347" img_size="full" onclick="custom_link" img_link_target="_blank" link="https://qureskincare.com/?snowball=SIGOURNEY57027"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] So… What Is Hypochlorous Acid? It may sound like something you’d find under a lab sink, but hypochlorous acid is actually a natural molecule your immune cells produce to fight off bacteria and inflammation. In skincare? It’s the goldilocks of actives: powerful enough to help with blemishes, irritation, sensitivity—even wound healing—but gentle enough for post-treatment skin, eczema, and daily use. Dermatologists like Dr. Shah and Dr. Maxfield are singing its praises on YouTube : “This is the right way to use hypochlorous acid in skincare,” says Dr. Shah, holding up Qure’s mist like it’s the last bottle of Evian in the apocalypse. “It’s genius in this bundle—perfect before micro-infusion or any treatment. It’s clean, clever, and makes so much sense.” He’s talking about the fact that Qure sells the mists as part of their famous micro-infusion DIY dermastamping ( more on that here ) and you can also use them with the Qure LED masks - so this was the perfect accompaniment. But actually the mist has many more uses than just fastidiously cleaning your dermal tools.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66348" img_size="full" onclick="custom_link" img_link_target="_blank" link="https://qureskincare.com/?snowball=SIGOURNEY57027"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
11-years-of-beauticate-a-love-letter: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""] Eleven years ago, I had a decision to make. I’d just launched a little passion project— Beauticate —while I was still working as Beauty & Health Director at Vogue Australia . At the time, it was nothing more than an after-hours obsession. I was staying up late, writing, editing, dreaming. I had no real business plan, just an overwhelming curiosity about people, beauty, rituals, and real life. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66472" img_size="full"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text] But from the start, something clicked. The site took off quickly. People were watching. Reading. Sharing. Brands I’d worked with over the years started reaching out, asking to advertise. And that’s when I found myself standing at a career crossroads: do I leave my dream job—one that flew me around the world to interview icons like Lady Gaga, Rihanna and Cate Blanchett—or do I risk it all to build something that feels deeply, personally mine? It was terrifying. But I knew I had to leap. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66589" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
goldfield-and-banks-silky-woods-cairns: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66550" img_size="full" css=""][vc_column_text css=""] That was the promise of the Silky Woods Sojourn , a new scent-inspired stay launched by Goldfield & Banks and Crystalbrook Riley , where guests can indulge in a luxurious, olfactory-focused escape (and literal oud-infused cocktails) of one of Australia’s most coveted perfumes— Silky Woods . [/vc_column_text][vc_column_text css=""] It's a rare opportunity to live out your own beauty editor fantasy, complete with a signature cocktail, a luxe sea-view room, and a take-home bottle of your new favourite scent. [/vc_column_text][vc_column_text css=""] But before the public got a whiff, I was invited behind the scenes—along with a coterie of editors and influencers—for a press trip that promised sensory immersion. And let me tell you: it delivered. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66612" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]
family-ski-holiday-to-japan: If you’re searching for a destination that blends world-class skiing, cultural immersion, and family-friendly fun, Japan delivers. Whether you’re travelling with toddlers, kids, or teens, you’ll find it all — from ski runs that feel like floating through a dream, to steaming hot springs, to up-close encounters with snow monkeys.
red-light-therapy-infraredi-review: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""] Welcome to my not-so-secret weapon: red light therapy —via wearable LED masks and my Infraredi Pro Max panel that now lives, unashamedly, in my bedroom. It’s the quiet overachiever of my wellness routine. One of those modalities you don’t think you need… until you do. And then? You’re hooked. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66664" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]
dyson-supersonic-r-hair-dryer-review: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""] And now, after a month of twice-weekly use, I can safely say: this is not just a hair dryer. This is Dyson engineering meets red carpet styling. And it's about to change how we blowdry, forever... Mind you, this is not the first time we have had a first peek at Dyson's innovative hair engineering at Beauticate. Back in 2016 when Dyson first revolutionised hair dryers, we put their OG supersonic hair dryer to the test (it looks almost retro now!). We were impressed then, and now, 9 years on, the Supersonic has had a glow up (and shrink down) or epic proportions. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66887" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]
qure-shower-filter-review: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66912" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""] Well. Let’s just say the rabbit hole of water filtration has once again swallowed me whole. After installing a whole-house water filter and the gold standard of drinking filters: reverse osmosis system in my kitchen (I used Water Flow Control ), I started wondering what else was lurking in my beauty routine—particularly in my shower . The same stream I was washing my face with, cleansing my body under, and yep, occasionally gulping from, could be full of invisible nasties: chlorine, copper, heavy metals, even microplastics. So when Qure launched their Shower Filter , I was all in. [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="66911" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]
magnesium-pools-wellness-design-australi: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="67011" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]From magnesium-rich plunge pools to steam-filled bathrooms that double as day spas, the way we design our homes is shifting. More than ever, we’re favouring calm, recovery, and a little bit of everyday indulgence. With help from the team at Blue Haven Pools (and having tested more than a few of these features myself), here’s how water is becoming the new wellness frontier—and why now is the time to design with flow in mind.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="67016" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]In a world where self-care has finally moved beyond bath bombs and face masks, water is quietly becoming the unsung hero of modern wellness design. Whether it’s magnesium-rich pools, mood-shifting showers, or plunge pools made for endorphin-chasing cold dips, our homes are transforming into personal sanctuaries—and water is leading the charge. “To me, wellness pools are about creating a space that rejuvenates your body and mind,” says Remonda Martinez, CEO of Blue Haven Pools . “Whether that’s through mineral water, heat therapy, or just the ability to unplug from the world while floating under the stars.” [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="67136" img_size="full" css=""][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text css=""]
bannisters-port-stephens-family-getaway: We decided to check out Bannisters Port Stephens — the newest boutique coastal hotel in the Bannisters family — for a much-needed Port Stephens family getaway.
winter-beauty-rituals: The fantasy? Me, glowing in the snow, swaddled in glamour. The reality? Cold, dry skin that needs beauty rituals to truly deliver. During my recent ski trip to Japan reminded me how important it is to winter-proof both skin and soul. Between blizzards and onsens, I discovered a silky shea butter that became my daily rescue. Back home, I recreated the vibe with smoky scents, buttery textures, and forest-inspired self-care.
qure-glowcase-review: Because while you could elbow your way to the checkout line for a Zimmermann dress you’ll wear twice, I’d argue the smarter investment is the face you present to the world. Your skin is your first impression. It speaks before you do. And when it’s glowing, lifted, and calm, you feel that too. Confidence is a beauty product you can’t buy, but good skin gets you halfway there. Which brings me to the steals happening over at Qure right now. Their Black Friday bundles are frankly outrageous, in the best way (They’re offering up to 57% off) and if you’ve ever been LED light-curious or micro-needling tempted, this is your sign.
reve-skin-beauty-annandale: Occupying a beautiful corner position on Booth and View Street, Rêve Skin + Beauty Annandale announces itself quietly but confidently. My eye was instantly drawn to the warm burgundy exterior - rich, elegant and inviting. As I stepped through the front door, adorned with an oversized bow for the festive season, I felt an immediate sense of ease. Not rushed. Not clinical. Just calm. Founder Meghann Nikola and her team greeted me warmly at the door, and before I had even begun my tour, a loyal client followed closely behind me, happily sharing how devoted she is to Meghann and how regularly she books her appointments. It felt like the ultimate endorsement - the kind of loyalty that can’t be manufactured.
how-to-get-the-most-out-of-active-skinca: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="57814" img_size="full"][vc_column_text] Ultraderm founder Pauline Valle [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
chic-sunscreens-hats:
Teaching sun safety is one of those parenting ideals that sounds serene in theory and plays out very differently in real life.

In my case, it involves Lulu clocking my intentions from across the pool, physically recoiling the moment sunscreen appears, and me becoming extremely selective about what I’m willing to fight over.

Which is why this guide exists.

These are the sunscreens I trust, the ones I’ll happily use on myself, and the ones Lulu will tolerate. Plus the hats that make sun protection feel chic rather than punitive.

Because if it looks good, it’s far more likely to happen.
sustainable-luxury-travel-australia-wild: There are not many places that can comfortably host three generations of a family, a long birthday lunch, a dozen excited children in pyjamas and a group of mothers drinking wine around a fire pit.Wild Luxury's Crane Lodge and Calabash Bay Lodge can. Over the years we've stayed at both properties in wildly different configurations. A multi-generational family gathering. A weekend away with Damien's parents. A mummy-and-daughter birthday sleepover for Lulu and her closest friends. And somehow, despite the shifting cast of characters, both lodges delivered the same thing every time: space to connect. Not just physically, but emotionally. The fact that they're also beautifully designed, deeply sustainable and set in some of New South Wales' most spectacular landscapes feels almost secondary. Crane Lodge, a sustainable luxury lodge overlooking Pittwater, designed to blend architecture with native Australian landscape. The new language of sustainable luxury travel in Australia doesn’t announce itself. It doesn’t ask for sacrifice or applause. It simply shows up in how a place is built, how it sits in the landscape, and how it allows people to gather, rest and reconnect without friction. That’s what I felt so clearly across two stays with Wild Luxury . If you’ve followed our approach to design-led travel - you’ll understand this lens. This wasn’t about escape. It was about how a place holds real life. And not solo, not romantically, not even quietly. These were stays with my extended family - which, for many families, requires a certain kind of house: with places to assemble but also enough spaces for time apart. A retreat for the in-laws far from the maddening cries of kids. Built for moments of peace and solitude that every parent craves.

how-to-nail-the-clean-girl-beauty-trend: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]Of course, the word ‘clean’ in beauty requires an acknowledgement of its problematic nature. Concepts of cleanliness have undeniably difficult historical, racist and sexist connotations. ‘Clean’ has also come to denote a whole world of unregulated ingredient ‘standards’ and largely unhelpful ‘free-from’ ingredient lists. Happily, though, the ‘clean girl beauty’ trend, for all that its title relies on a somewhat tricky word as well as limited concepts of age and gender, is really suggesting something far more desirable and accessible for any person who wants to partake – looking like the best everyday version of yourself. And to achieve that, it’s really about getting to know your own face. (For instance, do you have skin that needs more than a sheer wash of colour to look polished? Welcome to the club! And yes, this look can still be for you.) As Mecca ’s Head of Artistry Jennifer Horsley says, “you might still use a lot of different products to achieve the ‘natural‘ look – it’s really about finding products that work for you and your skin.” Let us guide you to finding them.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_separator][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
why-you-need-this-new-blush-in-your-make: [vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61634" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][vc_column_text] Holme Beauty Founder Hilary Holmes [/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
skyn-perth: [vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61666" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][vc_single_image image="62656" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]SKYN was founded by Catherine Lee; she knows skin. As well as being a qualified beauty therapist, she holds a Bachelor of Health Science (Dermal Therapies) and is a member of the Australian Society of Dermal Clinicians. In 2004, she established SKYN and realized her dream of having her own space in which she could help clients achieve that elusive ‘healthy glow’.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="62654" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61667" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]Catherine’s philosophy is that, with the right care and treatment, healthy skin is achievable for everyone. Treatments always utilize well-established dermal therapy techniques as well as the latest technological advancements in devices and skincare. She also places a strong emphasis on educating clients about proper skin care practices to help them maintain results outside of the clinic.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61669" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61670" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]“We begin each client’s SKYN journey with an in-depth consultation to assess their skin and their goals, and then design a bespoke treatment plan”, Catherine tells us. The options available at the clinic are far-reaching, so it is key to have a trusted expert guide you as to which services will meet your needs. At SKYN, these offerings include energy-based devices such as Erbium, IPL, and lasers (like CO2 and ND: Yag) for resurfacing and rejuvenation, microdermabrasion, chemical peels, PRP, and PDO threads, as well as cosmetic injectables including wrinkle relaxers, dermal fillers, and biostimulators. In-salon skincare includes industry-beloved cosmeceutical brands ZO Skin Health, Cosmedix, and Mesoesthetic. And out of all of those treatment options, what proves to be the most popular? Catherine says, “Depending on the time of the year, laser resurfacing and rejuvenation give such fantastic results. And of course, wrinkle relaxers are a year-round must-have for most SKYN clients”.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61671" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61672" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]Of course, as with any beauty treatment space, it’s not just about the results; client experience matters too, whether it’s the aesthetics of the salon or a sweet treat or two! SKYN’s design and fit-out were completed by Catherine’s “incredibly handy” partner, Matt. Highlights of the finished space include artwork by Antonio Mora and, of all places, the bathroom (often the most neglected room in many salons). As Catherine points out, “We have an amazing bathroom with a Tom Dixon light fitting, and it has become a real hub for clients taking post-treatment selfies!" To start or end your treatment on the right note, take advantage of the clinic’s Nespresso coffee machine, Madame Flavour teas, San Pellegrino sparkling water, and personalized SKYN chocolate lips.[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61675" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="62655" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]Given SKYN has been around since 2004, Catherine has seen many beauty fads come and go. Her focus is on cutting-edge technologies and products, but always those backed by sound science and clinical results rather than the latest social media trends. Catherine shares, “We are so proud to have many loyal clients who have been with us since the beginning. We have a very high rate of client retention and referrals, which is a great testament to our team. We are right there alongside each client on their skin journey and are as excited as they are to see the results we can achieve along the way. We want each client at SKYN to feel their individual needs are being addressed with care and professionalism at every single appointment.”[/vc_column_text][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61673" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_single_image image="61674" img_size="full" css_animation="fadeInUp"][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row][vc_column][vc_column_text]
built-by-damo-the-best-deck-on-the-block: As part of our backyard renovation, which will eventually include putting a pool in, we needed to move our deck to the other side of the backyard, as the pool is going where the current deck is. My husband Damien, being the DIY tragic that he is, said, “You had me at ‘project’”. As with any good DIY enthusiast, he grossly underestimated the scope, budget and time required to complete said project. He initially planned just to move the current deck to a new location, but after he found someone in the neighbourhood giving away several large timber posts, he got excited about the idea of making it more substantial. He then felt like the old deck would be too small for the big thick posts, so decided to increase the size of the deck and to take advantage of the space available there. Our local council regulations allow up to 20 square metres without a permit, (with some other constraints - such as floor height and roof height), so we stuck to those guidelines to get it done without the paperwork. He also wanted to put a roof on it, so we didn’t have to put the cover on the outside furniture when it rained. It quickly went from a simple ‘move the current deck’ to a ‘build an entirely custom indoor/outdoor living space’. We initially thought the deck move would take a few weekends. But not that’s not how it turned out. Spoiler alert: It took 8 months (Damo justified this timeline based on the change in scope and his weekend availability) …
why-your-tap-water-might-be-ruining-your: As a beauty journalist for the past two and a half decades, I’ve tested just about every serum, mask, and device promising to deliver better skin. But one of the biggest game-changers in my skincare routine hasn’t been a product at all—it’s been the water coming out of my tap. And a little Faucet Filter device from my friends at Qure (read on for your discount code!). I never thought I’d become a water filtration person. But once you go down the rabbit hole of what’s actually lurking in tap water, it’s hard to unsee it. It started with a deep dive into filtration for drinking water—after all, clean water is essential, right? So, I had Water Flow Control install a whole-house filtration system, then added an RO (reverse osmosis) filter under my sink for drinking water. But there was one tap I kept worrying about—my bathroom sink.
best-ai-image-generator-app-review-glam-: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AeQM4yL_VtA It's a question that's trending on Google right now: Q: Should I try AI-generated portraits? The answer in a nutshell? A: They’re fun, fantastical and hyper-flattering , but they also reveal what tech really thinks beauty looks like. And that’s where things get complicated. I fell down a rabbit hole. Not the kind involving late white rabbits or existential crises about time (although, sure, those too), but the kind paved with perfect skin, ballgowns, desert sunsets and very implausible poodles. It started with a casual test of an image generator app that lets you upload a few photos and choose your fantasy shoot settings: think Vogue Arabia meets Upper East Side old money meets Disney-on-a-desert-safari. And honestly? I was hooked. Leopard print, power suits, and surreal glamour: what AI thinks I should look like.
beauty-expo-australia-2025-for-founders-: This August, I’m heading to Beauty Expo Australia 2025 at ICC Sydney , and I’d love for you to come too— as my VIP guest . Or you can get tickets here .
qure-neck-decolletage-led-device:
Freckled, sun-soaked, and etched from years of side-sleeping (why must I be such a foetal-position girlie?), my décolletage has long been my beauty bugbear.

The truth is, no matter how many serums you slather or facials you book, if you’re skipping your neck and décolletage, you’re ignoring one of the first places to show signs of ageing.

And I should know. After two decades in beauty journalism, I’ve tried it all: expensive neck creams, sticky silicone pads (inevitably peeled off at 2am), collagen masks (lovely, but messy), IPL, lasers… but my neck and chest still frustrated me.

So when Qure sent me their Neck & Décolletage LED Device a few years ago, I jumped straight on that bandwagon. And honestly? It’s one of the few things that made a lasting difference.

This month, Qure is making it even better: purchase the Qure Face LED Mask in September and receive the Neck & Décolletage Device (valued at $470) for free . That’s over $500 worth of skincare technology in one hit.
ingredient-smart-beauty-swaps:
These are the thoughtfully formulated upgrades that made the cut in my real routine - the ones I keep reaching for because they feel good, look chic on the shelf, and most importantly? They work.

No preachy ingredient panic. Just some better-for-you options that you’ll actually want to use.
christmas-led-skincare-gift: This year, I’m campaigning for fewer novelty mugs, panic candles and vaguely passive-aggressive Secret Santa gifts, and more presents that actually do something. Preferably for your skin. Preferably while you’re lying down. Enter: LED. f you’ve been anywhere near a dermatologist’s office, a medi-spa, or the skincare-obsessed corner of the internet lately, you’ll know LED light therapy has well and truly crossed over from “nice clinic add-on” to serious at-home skincare investment. And for good reason. I’ve been testing Qure’s LED tech for months now, and quietly loving the ritual, the results, and the fact that it requires absolutely zero effort beyond putting a mask on and existing. Which is my favourite kind of skincare. But here’s the part that made me do a double take: Qure is still running one of the most generous offers I’ve seen this year. Buy the LED face mask and they’re literally giving you the neck device for free. Two LED devices for the price of one. Yes. Really.
why-i-swapped-my-whoop-for-an-ultrahuman:
Naturally, the next step was to start tracking it.

That’s how I fell into the world of wearable tech - and why, for a long time, I wore the Whoop band. I even went full Gwyneth and got the champagne-coloured strap with gold hardware, which looked chic until it started looking a bit… grotty. Foundation, sweat and God knows what else. Let’s just say if it were a handbag, it would’ve been demoted to “school run only.”
nightly-beauty-routine: By the time I get home, I don’t want silence. I want movement. Familiar chaos. Hello, ADHD. I want a clean face, no bra, a glass poured, emails half-checked, and Bravo on loud enough that I can hear women yelling while I move around the apartment. Candles lit because - why not? It’s not about switching off - it’s about switching gears. Especially now, with a kitten called Blueberry Muffin supervising proceedings and a month full of obligations already behind me. These nights aren’t about pretending I don’t love things. They’re about ritual, routine, and using products properly -many I’ve relied on for years, some newer, all genuinely part of my life. I won’t lie: I love products. I love experiencing the scents, textures, and most of all… the packaging. I am a marketer’s dream.
luxury-pool-design-trends-australia-2: Working with Blue Haven on our own dream pool gave me a front-row seat to the new era of aquatic luxury - think sculptural curves, textural tiles, magnesium water, and sunken lounges that wouldn’t look out of place at a five-star resort. "We’re seeing a big shift towards curves, minimalism, and spaces that feel like luxury resorts at home," says Remonda Martinez , CEO of Blue Haven Pools. "Think built-in lounges, seamless edges, smart lighting, and that indoor-outdoor flow. People are over the standard rectangle and want a pool that feels like a vibe. It’s all about integration." Whether you're planning a knockdown rebuild or revamping your existing space, here are the seven design-led trends redefining Australian luxury pool design in 2026 - many of which featured in our very own pool collaboration with Blue Haven.
last-minute-christmas-gifts: Gifts That Feel Considered, Even If You’re Buying Them This Week Curated by the Beauticate team, this is a calm edit of last-minute Christmas gifts chosen for their quality, thoughtfulness and ease. Nothing frantic. Nothing obvious. Just pieces that feel considered, even if they’re bought at the last minute. Because good taste isn’t about timing - it’s about intention.
what-to-watch-and-listen-to-over-the-bre:
Curated for different moods and ways of listening, this is an easy edit of podcast and vodcast episodes to watch or listen to at your own pace.

Sigourney Cantelo

Founder & Host, Beautiful Inside by Beauticate
lazy-girls-guide-summer-entertaining: Since installing a pool (and promptly running out of money), life has become entirely about hosting. Casual swims turn into grazing boards. “Just popping over” becomes a four-hour affair. And because I’m equal parts busy, lazy and deeply committed to things looking nice, I’ve developed a series of entertaining hacks that I’m more than happy to share. At this point in summer, the diary is a revolving door of different groups coming through, and I’m officially in repeat-host mode. These are the ideas, shortcuts and small details that make summer entertaining look effortless - even when it very much isn’t.
pool-accessories-that-changed-our-lives: What he didn’t realise was that we were about to get our lives back. If you’ve followed our pool journey , you’ll know we approached it as a lifestyle project, not just a build - very much in line with the design-led thinking we explored in The Mondrian Gold Coast Is the Luxe Coastal Escape You Deserve .
red-light-therapy-hair-thinning: I first experienced it after my babies were born - that slow, confronting postpartum shedding that turns every shower into a small emotional reckoning. Then again during periods of intense stress, when lifting clumps of hair from the drain felt genuinely soul-destroying. I tried everything: shampoos, sprays, supplements, scalp massages, even prayer. If you’ve been there, you know it’s not vanity. It’s grief. Hair carries identity, health and confidence. Watching it thin can feel quietly devastating. My husband Damien’s experience has been different, but just as familiar. His hair has been gradually thinning for years. Nothing dramatic. Just that slow, creeping change many men notice and rarely talk about. That uncertainty is what makes hair loss so unsettling. You don’t wake up bald. You just wake up one day more aware.
qure-micro-infusion-system-review: When we called on our Beauticate community to trial the Qure Micro-Infusion System , we weren’t looking for influencers. We were looking for real people with real skin concerns - texture, fine lines, sun damage and dullness - willing to document their experience honestly. Four readers were selected across different skin types and concerns, each committing to the eight-week trial. No scripts. No paid endorsements. Just the System - and the results.
dyson-v16-review: If this Dyson V16 review proves anything, it is that I never thought I’d enjoy vacuuming. Let alone want to actually clean the damn thing. Yet suddenly, here I am, one Thursday night, lovingly taking it apart and detailing it with an old toothbrush, and, strangely, not minding one little bit. This is not who I used to be. Vacuum maintenance was always my hard no. The worst job in the house. The scissors. The hair. The gag reflex. The sense that I was wasting my one precious life dealing with something that should have worked better in the first place. But this machine changed the dynamic. That is really the heart of this Dyson V16 review: this machine removed the friction from a job I had always hated. The Dyson V16 Piston Animal Submarine quietly turned a job I loathed into something I… enjoy. Deeply. Repeatedly. Possibly too much.
breast-cancer-beauty-routine: Breast cancer can impact every part of life, including identity and self-image. This story shares one woman’s experience navigating diagnosis, treatment and the small rituals that helped her feel like herself again. Sigourney x
systeme-bio-plus-oil-serum-review:
TL;DR: Is the Systeme Bio+ Oil Serum worth it?

Yes. Four Beauticate readers trialled it over two weeks and every one reported real results - improved hydration, visible glow and softer fine lines within days. The lightweight dry-oil formula works morning and night, under makeup, and across different skin types including sensitive skin.

When we called on our Beauticate community to trial the Système Bio+ Oil Serum , we weren't looking for influencers. (And yes, we have a discount code for you - BEAUTICATE20 at checkout!)

We were looking for real people with real skin concerns - dryness, dullness, fine lines, and loss of radiance - willing to document their experience honestly.

Our readers were selected across different skin types and lifestyles, each committing to the trial.

No scripts.

No paid endorsements.

Just the serum - and the results.
beauticate-collective-editors-experts: There's something quietly powerful about a trusted recommendation. Not the kind that shouts. The kind that lands softly. The one you save, screenshot, and come back to. At Beauticate , that has always been the goal. To cut through the noise and bring you what actually works. What's worth your time. What feels good, not just looks good . Building this platform has been, among many things, a long and deliberate search for the right women to be part of it. Some I met in the magazine world over two decades ago. Some I discovered through their work. Some have been in my life for so long that formalising it almost feels funny: Mon has been colouring my hair for five years, Kristin has been styling Beauticate shoots for nearly a decade, and Kate , Rae and Jocelyn were among the very first women I interviewed for Beauticate, twelve years ago, when all of this was still a passion project I was building after hours at Vogue. What we are announcing today is not an influencer program. Nor is it a paid partnership arrangement dressed up as editorial. It’s something I’ve been quietly building toward for years. The Beauticate Collective is a curated group of editors , experts and tastemakers . Women at the top of their fields whose opinions carry weight because they have lived it , tested it and r efined it over years, not weeks. Not built on follower count - built on credibility , taste and trust . Think of it as an extension of Beauticate's editorial voice: a network of contributors who will share personal product edits, expert features, their real routines and rituals, and help shape what lands in the Beauticate.Shop (Eeep! More on that soon!). I am so excited for this. I hope you love The Collective as much as I do. Sigourney x
kate-waterhouse-style-beauty:
Back then, Kate welcomed us into her home - an apartment just down the road from this beautiful home - and we created some beauty videos together (flashback to How to do A Smokey Eye and How She Prepped Her Skin - we haven’t aged a bit - ha ha!)

So when the time came to bring a Style Editor into the Beauticate fold, there was really only one person.

Now, Kate brings that same generosity and considered eye to Beauticate in an official capacity: sharing her take on modern style, wardrobe staples and the pieces that truly work in real life. We visited her at her beautiful beachside home in Sydney, where she lives with her husband, two daughters and their much-loved Cobberdog. The space feels like an extension of her personal style. Thoughtful, elegant and quietly luxurious, yet warm and completely liveable. The kind of home that looks considered, but never overdone.
brow-mistakes-most-women-make: For women in their 40s and 50s dealing with thinning brows, patchy growth, greying hairs or the long-term fallout of a particularly aggressive '90s waxing phase, working out where to even start can feel overwhelming. And it’s something that makeup artist and beauty therapist Simone Aspinall sees every day. The trick is not about chasing trends or creating a completely different shape, she says, but rather, it’s about enhancing what's already there and creating balance, softness and structure in a way that looks effortless. Here, she breaks down the mistakes she sees most often, and exactly what to do instead.
